v-0.1.0.description:
# First official "release".

There are still prone to be issues as I have yet to retest after implementing a decent amount of code. However, this should still fix a considerable amount of issues from the last patch.

Installation instructions are on the `README`.

Any bugs or issues *should be reported*, and thank you for trying out my AP!
v-0.1.0.title: Laika AP Release V 0.1.0

v-0.1.1.description: Massive improvements over the previous release. Too many to list, however I will name the most relevant ones. - UI fixes. The UI now properly scales accordingly based on the user's resolution. (Thank you ndubs103 for discovering this!) - Map and Cassettes have been overhauled completely. Both will consistently function as intended and function even when received as an item. - The scene after the completion of _Quest: Rage and Sorrow_ now is completely skipped. This fix will be applied to other long cutscenes (Such as the one in The Big Tree after defeating the boss), as well as other potential candidates in the future. - _Quest: Radio Silence_ is significantly refined to have reconciliation in case the player acquires _Dash: Bike Upgrade_ . It also now updates accordingly depending on if the player is lacking (or has) the harpoon AP items at any point. - Both quests _Radio Silence_ and _The Big Tree_ now have additional locations to check. Radio Silence now have the antennas as checkable locations while The Big Tree now has floors destroyed. This should alleviate long downtime in those areas where checks aren't as plentiful. - Maya's Pendant no longer spams the player when acquired. Previously, it would constantly pop up informing the player that it has been found. - Tons, and I mean _tons_ of improvements to the logic. It is far from perfect, but this should prevent key items from being in locations that were previously inaccessible. - Key item locations now automatically are claimed if you manage to complete the quest before visiting that key item's location. Example: Acquiring If the player acquires _Key Item: Petey's Letter_ from an AP item received and turns in _Quest: The Prophecy_ before checking the location, it will instead automatically send out both checks instantaneously instead of requiring the player to go pickup Petey's Letter in its vanilla spot. That's all for now! Tons of more improvements are on the way, and this should be one step closer to having a fully functional/stable Laika: Aged Through Blood archipelago. :) Stay safe out there bikers, and hope to see you in the wasteland!
v-0.1.1.title: Laika AP Release V 0.1.1

v-0.1.2.description: # Laika: Aged Through Blood Archipelago v1.2 Update Notes The wasteland has grown a little stranger, a little cleaner, and a lot more connected. This update is a major support, polish, and stability pass for the Laika Archipelago randomizer. It adds Universal Tracker support, improves item and quest logic, cleans up the client, and fixes several edge cases that could make the road rougher than intended. # Universal Tracker Support! Universal Tracker support is now included. Players can now use Universal Tracker to view Laika’s world map, see randomized check locations, and follow their current area as they travel across the wasteland. The tracker includes maps for all major regions, including Where We Live, Where Birds Lurk, Where All Was Lost, The Big Tree, Floating City, and more. The tracker now updates based on the player’s current in-game region. The opening tutorial route is also handled separately, so the tracker should stay on the tutorial map at the start of the game and then switch to normal areas after Rage and Sorrow. Universal Tracker’s `/explain` command is also supported for Laika locations, giving players a clearer breakdown of what they still need for quests, bosses, key items, map pieces, and other checks. # New and Expanded Location Support Several missing or previously untracked checks have been added to the randomizer. Newly supported checks include things like: ```Key Item: Radio Transmitter Key Item: Iris Key Item: Large Seed Key Item: Moon Blossom Key Item: Pads Blueprint: Rocket Launcher Additional cassette rewards Additional weapon crafting materials ``` Radio Transmitter is now properly treated as a Where Birds Lurk pickup and is required for the final progression path. Several cassette tapes that were previously bundled or missing from tracker logic are now represented as proper Archipelago checks. # Improved Logic and Progression Progression logic has been tightened across the world so the randomizer better matches what Laika can actually do. Notable logic updates include: ```Diplomacy now requires all three Pit Keys and the Mountainheart Card. A Caterpillar Made of Sadness now also requires the Mountainheart Card and all three Pit Keys. Clean Your Beak and Flute Cleaning Brush have stricter quest requirements. Radio Transmitter is now part of the late-game progression chain. Two-Beak God now requires the Radio Transmitter. Several cassette, key item, and quest checks were moved to more accurate requirements. ``` This should reduce cases where the tracker or server thinks something is in logic before the player can actually do it. # Better Quest Item Handling Quest items should behave more reliably when received from Archipelago. This update improves handling for AP-granted quest items that are later turned in to NPCs, including extra protection for quest chains involving: ```Iris Rainbow Pebble Birthday Invitation Jar Filled With Bugs Pads Moon Blossom Heartglaze Flower Jakob’s Ashes ``` The goal is to let vanilla quest logic still run correctly while preventing randomized quest items from being permanently lost, duplicated, or missed as checks. Heartglaze Flower still has special handling because the game expects the physical flower pickup to happen in a specific boss-room sequence. # Final Goal / Release Fix Goal completion should now be reported at the actual final ending sequence. Previously, defeating the final boss could send the location check, but the Archipelago game might not properly mark the slot as complete for release/goal purposes. v1.2 now reports completion during the final bomb/ending sequence, around the point where the credits are reached. This should fix cases where a player finished the game but the server did not release them. # Recent Activity and Hint Improvements The in-game Archipelago activity log has been improved. Hints should now display more cleanly, and duplicate bad hint messages like: `Item -1 at Location -1` should be suppressed when they come from tracker/text-client chat echoes. Item coloring has also been updated so newer key items, including Radio Transmitter, display with the correct category color. # Title Screen and Connection Polish The title screen Archipelago setup has received more polish. The AP connection panel, save-slot behavior, connection status text, DeathLink settings, Death Amnesty settings, and per-slot AP state handling have all been cleaned up. The mod now does a better job of binding AP settings to the correct save slot, reconnecting cleanly, and disconnecting when appropriate. # Weapon Mode and YAML Improvements The player YAML has been cleaned up to feel more release-ready and less prototype-like. Weapon Mode is now explained more clearly: ```direct: weapons are sent as complete weapons crafting: most weapons are split into blueprint + unique material ``` DeathLink and Death Amnesty options are also documented more clearly, including the Death Amnesty count range. The old “Coco” item name has also been cleaned up to the more player-friendly: `Ingredient: Coconut Milk` # Code Refactor and Stability Work A large internal cleanup was done on the C# mod and APWorld. The C# client was split into cleaner files for: ```Connection handling Packet handling Item grants Location checks DeathLink Universal Tracker Reconciliation Overlay display Title screen patches Quest patches Inventory patches Boss patches Cassette patches Map patches ``` The APWorld was also split and cleaned up with separate files for item pools, Universal Tracker setup, and logic explanations. This should make future fixes and features much easier to maintain. Phew! I believe that's everything? I'll try to make patches have less buffer in between to ensure the game has proper functionality after issues are discovered. There are still issues which are listed in the pins of the current issues on the Archipelago Discord. Please check there and ensure your issue isn't already reported. Otherwise, see you in the Wasteland! Please enjoy this new and improved patch!
v-0.1.2.title: Laika AP Release V 0.1.2
